content.full_text:
92-148
2

CITY OF MOUNTAIN VIEW

Office of the City Attorney • 500 Castro Street • Post Office Box 7540 • Mountain View, California 94039-7540
415-903-6303 • FAX 415-967-4215

VIA FAX AND SURFACE MAIL

May 26, 1992

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

ATTENTION: Blanca M. Breeze, Counsel, Legal Division

Re: Advice Letter No. A-92-7540--Mountain View Councilmember Dena Bonnell

Dear Ms. Breeze:

I have received the above Advice Letter, dated May 8, 1992, and forwarded it to
Councilmember Bonnell. In discussing the letter with Councilmember Bonnell,
she pointed out one assumption not in the submitted facts, which may affect the
advice given. The error appears on page 7 of the advice letter.

Councilmember Bonnell pointed out that the unpaid fuel taxes collected are not
shown as an asset on the Company's books, but as a liability. Only the Internal
Revenue Service, Franchise Tax board, and State Board of Equalization (the Taxing
Agencies) consider these amounts assets; they are considered liabilities by the
business.

The taxes are liabilities because they are due from and are paid by Valley Oil Co. at
the time of delivery of the taxed product. The customer actually pays for the
delivered product some time after delivery. The business neither controls or
derives economic benefit from the eventual collection of tax payments. Upon
collection, the company reimburses itself for funds it was required to advance by
virtue of the tax collectors' compulsion. If the customer defaults, the business is
entitled to a refund of taxes paid on the defaulted account, after reasonable efforts to
collect have failed. In such circumstances, the business neither has "control" or
derives "economic benefit" from the taxes eventually collected.

Recycled Paper

Scott Hallabrin
ATT: Blanca Breeze
May 26, 1992
Page 2

I request that you review the advice with this information in mind. I am available
to answer any questions or provide any further information which you may need to
respond to this question. I can be reached during normal business hours at
(415) 903-6303 or before 8 a.m. or after 5 p.m. at (415) 903-6398. Please reply to the Fax
number indicated on the letterhead.

Thank you for your consideration.

Very truly yours,

P. Lawrence Klose
City Attorney

cc: Councilmember Bonnell, City Council, CM

sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of Mountain View is requesting reconsideration of FPPC Advice Letter A-92-7540 regarding Councilmember Dena Bonnell, specifically challenging the characterization of unpaid fuel taxes as assets rather than liabilities on a company's books.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence/request for reconsideration, not an FPPC response. The document references a prior FPPC advice letter (A-92-7540) but this letter itself is from the City Attorney requesting the FPPC to review that advice. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in this document.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney responds to FPPC Advice Letter A-92-7540 dated May 8, 1992, requesting that the FPPC reconsider its analysis based on a factual correction: unpaid fuel taxes collected should be treated as liabilities rather than assets from the business's accounting perspective, as they represent funds the company must advance and reimburse itself for upon collection.
